non_compete.title: State vs. Silicon Valley Corp (2023)
non_compete.ruling: Non-compete clauses exceeding 1 year in the technology sector are deemed overly restrictive and unenforceable unless the employee possesses highly proprietary trade secrets that directly threaten the core business model.

intellectual_property.title: Dev v. MegaCorp (2021)
intellectual_property.ruling: Intellectual property generated by an employee using personal equipment outside of standard working hours cannot automatically be assigned to the employer unless directly related to the employer's active R&D pipeline.

equity_tax.title: IRS vs. Startup Inc (2019)
equity_tax.ruling: Equity grants (RSUs or Stock Options) must be clearly delineated with a vesting schedule (e.g., 4-year vest, 1-year cliff) to avoid immediate severe tax liabilities upon signing. Immediate unvested grants are subject to ordinary income tax.
